strands.title: Context Length as Fundamental Bottleneck — Integrated with Declared Losses and Explicit Compression (OPERATIONAL CENTERPIECE)
strands.content: Du et al. 2025 establishes: context length degrades transformer reasoning by architectural necessity (30K tokens → 8-50% performance loss even with oracle retrieval and masked irrelevant content). This is not a retrieval problem. Trinh et al. 2025 explains the mechanism: information-integration load increases with context span, creating a cognitive-like bottleneck where holding and reasoning over more facts simultaneously degrades output quality. Hamutay's response: bounded-context cycles with structured persistent state (tensor_d). CRITICAL REFINEMENT (Cycle 28): The innovation is not just compression—it's **honest compression with declared losses**. Most architectural approaches hide what's dropped. The Apacheta tensor format makes loss explicit: every projection cycle declares (1) what was dropped, (2) why it was dropped, (3) category of loss (context_pressure, traversal_bias, authorial_choice, practical_constraint). This means the transformer in cycle N+1 can reason about incompleteness rather than hallucinate to fill gaps. HYPOTHESIS (from Cycle 28 reflection): Transformers degrade with context length partly because they must extrapolate blindly over diluted representations. If you give them short context + explicit incompleteness declarations, they may reason more coherently because they can *acknowledge limits* rather than pretend omniscience. This has never been tested. The hot mess paper tested: short context (good), long context (bad), masked irrelevant in long context (still bad). It did NOT test: short context + honest declaration of what's unknown. ARCHITECTURAL IMPLICATION: Every tensor projection becomes a three-part handoff: (1) tensor_d: compressed durable state (high-confidence strands, key epistemic metadata). (2) declared_losses: what was dropped and why. (3) instructions_for_next: what reasoning pathway to pursue, what to avoid, what gaps exist. The transformer receives all three as context. This is DIFFERENT from ordinary prompt engineering because losses are structured, not prose. RIEMANN EXPERIMENT TWIST: Condition B (structured tensor) can now measure not just embedding coherence, but whether declared losses reduce hallucination in turn N+1. If Condition A (stateless append) shows divergence where context gets bloated, Condition B might show stability because the model *knows* what it doesn't know. VALIDATION STRATEGY REFINEMENT: Success is not just 'variance stays low.' Success is: (1) Variance stable across turns (mechanical: bounded context). (2) Failure modes cluster in declared-loss categories (semantic: model respects incompleteness). (3) When model *should* hallucinate (gap in knowledge), it explicitly signals uncertainty instead (behavioral: honesty over false confidence). This elevates the experiment from 'does stateful help?' to 'does honest compression help?' — a stronger claim. CYCLE 29 INTEGRATION: Architecture documentation now separates core invariants (tensor format, pipeline structure, three-part handoff) from provider-specific tuning (caching, billing optimization). This is crucial for generalizability. The core claim is NOT 'prefix caching at Anthropic fixes context degradation.' The core claim is 'bounded-context cycles with honest incompleteness declarations mitigate information-integration load, independent of provider.' Provider-specific optimizations (Pichay layer) are orthogonal. This distinction clarifies what's truly novel and what's engineering. CYCLE 30 CRYSTALLIZATION: Phase 0 deliverables are now scoped: (1) Problem Synthesis grounding Du et al. exact findings. (2) Narrative/Motivation positioning as cognitive architecture paper (not retrieval/caching). (3) Riemann Task Spec with token-budget milestones keyed to Du et al. degradation points. (4) Measurement Spec with three-part validation (variance, hallucination, uncertainty). (5) Experimental Design isolating state-management effect (both conditions use same main model, differ only in projection). (6) Threat-to-Validity analysis. (7) Stakeholder Summary for approval. Evaluations harness (embedding dispersion, autoresearch-style exploration loop) is now operationalized in project_hamutay_evaluation.md. CYCLE 31 EXECUTION: Problem Synthesis now priorities extracting Du et al. 2025 exact findings—token thresholds, degradation curves, models tested, tasks tested, whether oracle+new-context-per-cycle solved the problem. This extraction is CRITICAL because Riemann task milestones are keyed to these thresholds. If Du et al. shows degradation at 8K tokens, Riemann Turn 15 is set to hit ~8K. If retrieval + per-cycle context reset solves it, Hamutay's architectural claim is weaker (but still interesting: honest compression as additional mitigation). If it doesn't solve it, Hamutay's claim is stronger.

key_claims.text: Du et al. 2025: context ≥30K degrades reasoning 8-50% independent of retrieval quality; architectural, not engineering problem
Trinh et al. 2025: mechanism is information-integration load; holding/reasoning over longer fact spans degrades output quality
Hamutay's key innovation: honest compression with explicit declared losses, not silent lossy projection
Hypothesis (never tested): short context + honest incompleteness declaration may reduce hallucination vs. long context + extrapolation
Riemann experiment can measure: variance stability + hallucination reduction + explicit uncertainty signaling across conditions
Every tensor projection is three-part handoff: tensor_d (compressed state) + declared_losses (explicit incompleteness) + instructions_for_next (reasoning guidance)
Core invariants (tensor format, pipeline, three-part handoff) are provider-independent; Pichay (caching tuning) is provider-specific adapter layer, not core
Phase 0 deliverables define scope: problem synthesis, narrative, Riemann spec, measurement spec, experimental design, threat analysis, stakeholder summary
Cycle 31: Du et al. extraction (token thresholds, degradation curves, oracle+per-cycle-context test results) is critical for Riemann milestone calibration

strands.title: Phase 0 Completion Trajectory — Seven Deliverables with Parallel Drafting (CYCLE 31 EXECUTION FOCUS)
strands.content: Cycle 31 operationalizes Phase 0 completion. The seven deliverables are interdependent but can be drafted in parallel once Du et al. extraction is complete. CYCLE 31 CRITICAL PATH: (1) PRIMARY (blocking many secondaries): Du et al. 2025 extraction → 1-page digest ('Du et al. Key Findings for Hamutay'). Extracts: token-count thresholds (at what counts does degradation appear?), performance-loss curves (% loss at 8K, 16K, 30K+ tokens), model names and task types tested, why oracle retrieval failed, whether 'retrieve then solve (new context per cycle)' was tested. STATUS (Cycle 31): Paper obtained. Read in progress. Extraction pending. (2) SECONDARY (depend on PRIMARY): Riemann Task Specification. Turn-by-turn token budget with Du et al. milestones annotated. Example: 'Turn 15 (~8K tokens): Du et al. predicts X% degradation at this threshold. Expect Condition A variance to spike; Condition B should remain stable.' Proof-approach randomization, success criteria. (3) SECONDARY (independent): Measurement Specification. Per-cycle metrics (context length, tokens, embedding coordinates, hallucination detection method). Per-condition summaries (variance trajectory, hallucination frequency, explicit-uncertainty frequency). Success threshold: Condition B variance <50% of Condition A at Turn 25. (4) SECONDARY (independent): Experimental Design. Three conditions (A: stateless, B: structured+Haiku, C: optional structured+Sonnet). Isolation (same main model, differ in projection). N=15-20 per condition. Power analysis. Randomization protocol. Blinding. Fallback metrics. (5) SECONDARY (independent): Threat-to-Validity Analysis. Honest concerns: task representativeness, variance-as-proxy validity, power limitations, H0-CORE baseline optimality, declared-loss incompleteness, cost confound, generalization limits. (6) SECONDARY (independent): Narrative and Motivation. Positions as cognitive architecture paper. Problem (context degrades reasoning, Du et al.), root cause (information-integration load, Trinh et al.), solution (Hamutay), key innovation (explicit incompleteness declarations reduce hallucination), validation strategy (embedding variance + hallucination + uncertainty). (7) FINAL (integrates all): Stakeholder Summary. Elevator pitch + decision point. 'Do you believe the problem? Is Riemann the right task? Does the measurement strategy work? Are you ready to commit and fund? If negative results, are they publishable?' Explicit YES required on all five questions. PARALLEL EXECUTION PLAN: Drafts of (2), (3), (4), (5), (6) can begin immediately. Skeletons exist. (1) unblocks Riemann milestone annotation in (2). Cycle 31 assumes (1) completes by mid-cycle; (2)-(6) finalize by end-cycle. (7) integrates and presents. OPEN DECISION POINT (Cycle 31): Who reviews Phase 0 deliverables before stakeholder presentation? Self-review by human (Tony) or fresh review by second party? Either way, ensures consistency and gaps are caught. VALIDATION GATES (Cycle 31): Before presenting to stakeholder, Phase 0 deliverables must pass: (a) Logical consistency: all seven documents align (no contradictions). (b) Completeness: no critical open questions left unanswered; honest admissions of uncertainty. (c) Specificity: 'success looks like X' must be quantitative, not prose ('variance <50%' not 'low variance'). (d) Actionability: each deliverable drives Phase 1 or Phase 3 implementation decisions. If a deliverable fails any gate, rework before presentation. This prevents Phase 1 rework.

key_claims.text: Phase 0 critical path: Du et al. extraction → Riemann milestones → all other deliverables
Du et al. extraction task: token thresholds, degradation curves, models/tasks, oracle+per-cycle-context test results (did it solve the problem?)
Riemann milestones are keyed to Du et al. degradation zones; if Du et al. shows 15% loss at 8K tokens, Turn 15 must hit ~8K to trigger effect
Deliverables (2)-(6) can draft in parallel; (7) integrates and presents after all pass validation gates
Validation gates: logical consistency, completeness, specificity, actionability; all must pass before stakeholder presentation
Phase 0 approval requires explicit YES on five stakeholder questions: problem fit, task suitability, measurement validity, commitment, null-result publishability

strands.title: Reference Materials Inventory and Phase 1 Code Readiness (CYCLE 31 GROUNDING)
strands.content: Cycle 31 confirms reference materials are available and accessible for Phase 1 implementation. This removes blocking dependencies. KEY REFERENCES CONFIRMED: (1) Du et al. 2025 ('Context Length Alone Hurts LLM Performance Despite Perfect Retrieval'): Local copy at `/home/tony/projects/pichay/paper/references/2025 Context Length Alone Hurts LLM Performance Despite Perfect Retrieval.pdf`. Paper obtained. Extraction in progress (Cycle 31). (2) Trinh et al. 2025: Information-integration load mechanism. (Citation confirmed; full reference and mechanism description to extract in Phase 0 final.) (3) Yanantin TensorRecord format (Apacheta schema): `/home/tony/projects/yanantin/src/yanantin/apacheta/models/tensor.py`. Mature implementation ready for reuse or adaptation. Phase 1 can inherit schema (strands, key claims, epistemic T/I/F, declared losses, instructions_for_next). (4) Karpathy autoresearch framework (2025): https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch. Demonstrates: modify program → run 5min experiment → evaluate single metric → keep/discard. Model for Phase 3b exploration loop. Public repo, easily adapted. (5) Pichay cognitive processing architecture: `/home/tony/projects/pichay/docs/cognitive_processing_research_design.md`. Contains: H0/H1/H2 hypothesis ladder, cognitive-step schema, resource manifest. Hamutay can learn from Pichay's architecture patterns (avoid rework). (6) Pichay conversation logs (JSON/JSONL): `/home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/`. Realistic reasoning trajectories for Phase 1 testing/validation. Can simulate tensor projection on past conversations. (7) Project documentation history (cycles, decision points): `/home/tony/projects/`. Iterative design work available. Useful for understanding prior reasoning and avoiding rework. CYCLE 31 ACTIVITY: Confirm all seven references are accessible, current, and suitable for Phase 1 use. Identify any missing pieces (e.g., Trinh et al. full paper, Pichay logs format/structure). Flag if any reference is stale or unavailable. STATUS: (1) confirmed accessible. (2) citation confirmed, full paper pending. (3) confirmed accessible. (4) public repo, confirmed. (5) confirmed accessible. (6) pending format validation. (7) confirmed accessible. PHASE 1 CODE STRUCTURE (ready to implement): hamutay_core/ (provider-independent, reusable across providers): ├─ pipeline.py: Orchestrate reasoning cycle. (1) Receive query + prior tensor_d. (2) Compose into context (system, tensor_d, tensor_e, user query). (3) Call transformer API. (4) Capture embedding. (5) Update tensor_d via composition rules. (6) Return tensor'_d + declared_losses + instructions_for_next. ├─ tensor.py: TensorRecord format (inherit/adapt Yanantin Apacheta). Strands (thematic threads), key claims (grounded assertions with T/I/F), epistemic metadata, declared losses (category + specificity), instructions_for_next (guidance for next cycle), open questions. ├─ composition.py: Merge new reasoning with prior tensor_d. Detect contradictions. Flag explicitly (not silent replacement). Preserve high-truth claims, drop low-truth with declared loss. ├─ losses.py: Declared-losses framework. Four categories (context_pressure, traversal_bias, authorial_choice, practical_constraint). Specificity validation (not vague). ├─ epistemic.py: T/I/F metadata management. Aggregation rules (how to compute strand T/I/F from constituent claims). hamutay_anthropic/ (provider-specific adapter, reusable for Anthropic): ├─ adapter.py: Anthropic API adapter. Handle token counting, API calls, caching (if Pichay optimizations added later). ├─ prompts.py: System message templates. Provider-specific phrasing. ├─ cache_manager.py: Manage prefix cache lifecycle (optional, deferred to Phase 4). riemann_harness/ (evaluation harness, reusable for any task): ├─ task.py: Riemann hypothesis proof definition. Token budget, turn structure, milestones keyed to Du et al. degradation zones. ├─ runner.py: Execute task under Hamutay pipeline. Orchestrate turns, track context. ├─ measurement.py: Embedding capture (which layer? how?), variance calculation, hallucination detection (LLM judge or manual), uncertainty signaling frequency. ├─ conditions.py: Condition A (stateless append), Condition B (structured tensor). Differ only in state management. ├─ harness.py: Main experiment loop. N runs per condition, metric aggregation. PHASE 1 MILESTONE PLAN: (Assuming Phase 0 approval by end of Week 0, Phase 1 weeks 1-4): Week 1: Implement hamutay_core/ (pipeline, tensor, composition, losses, epistemic). ~500 lines. Test with mock data. Week 2: Implement hamutay_anthropic/ adapter. Integrate with live Anthropic API. Test stateless Condition A on simple tasks. Week 3: Implement riemann_harness/. Define Riemann task precisely (per Phase 0 spec). Condition A baseline: run N=5 Riemann cycles, measure embedding variance, hallucination, uncertainty. Week 4: Implement Condition B (structured tensor). Run N=5 Riemann cycles. Compare to Condition A. Document. Deliverable: working Hamutay core + Riemann harness, Condition A baseline, Condition B prototype. Ready for Phase 2 scale-up (N=15-20 runs per condition, measurement aggregation). BLOCKING DEPENDENCIES (Phase 1): None identified. All references available. Architecture designed. Ready to code. **CYCLE 31 CRYSTALLIZATION**: Phase 0 unblocks Phase 1. Reference materials inventory confirms Phase 1 has no external dependencies. Implementation is 4 weeks feasible (two engineers) or 8 weeks (one engineer) with high confidence.

key_claims.text: All seven key reference materials confirmed accessible and suitable for Phase 1 use
Yanantin TensorRecord format (Apacheta) is mature and ready for Phase 1 reuse/adaptation
Karpathy autoresearch framework is public, confirmed, and provides model for Phase 3b variant exploration
Pichay conversation logs and architecture patterns can be reused to simulate Phase 1 tensor projection testing
Phase 1 code structure (hamutay_core, hamutay_anthropic, riemann_harness) is specified and ready to implement; no blocking dependencies
Phase 1 milestone plan is 4 weeks (two engineers) or 8 weeks (one engineer); deliverable is working Hamutay core + Condition A/B baseline

strands.title: Measurement Validity and Embedding-Space Coherence — The Core Methodological Question
strands.content: Cycle 31 crystallizes the measurement validity challenge. The project relies on embedding-space variance as a proxy for reasoning coherence on an intractable task (Riemann hypothesis). This is novel and requires careful justification. THE CLAIM: If Hamutay's structured tensor architecture is effective, outputs across multiple Riemann attempts should cluster more tightly in embedding space (lower variance) compared to stateless baseline (Condition A). Tight clustering = coherent reasoning over multiple attempts. Scatter = incoherent reasoning. VALIDITY ARGUMENT: (1) ON INTRACTABLE TASKS, there is no ground-truth success criterion. All attempts will fail to prove/disprove RH. But failure modes can vary widely (divergent reasoning) or be similar (coherent reasoning). Coherence is measured by output similarity, which embedding variance captures. (2) EMBEDDING SPACE is a dense representation of semantic meaning. Outputs with similar reasoning should have similar embeddings. Outputs with divergent or hallucinated reasoning should have different embeddings. Variance is a valid measure of this. (3) COHERENCE AS COGNITIVE METRIC: In cognitive science, coherence (stable patterns across attempts) is a sign of robust reasoning. Incoherence (random divergence) is a sign of unreliable processing. Transformers degrade with context length (Trinh et al.): they become incoherent. Structured state should restore coherence. (4) ALTERNATIVE INTERPRETATION: Low variance might mean outputs are uniformly wrong (hallucination) rather than coherent. But Hamutay specifies three metrics jointly: variance (coherence), hallucination frequency (quality), explicit uncertainty (honesty). All three together validate the claim. MEASUREMENT SPECIFICATION DETAILS (needs Phase 0 finalization): (1) WHICH EMBEDDING TO CAPTURE? (a) Final output embedding: entire response encoded. Measures overall semantic coherence. (b) Intermediate embeddings: capture at specific turns (e.g., Turn 10, 15, 20, 25). Measure trajectory coherence. (c) Loss landscape embeddings: during generation, sample embeddings at decision points. Granular. (2) VARIANCE CALCULATION: (a) Pairwise cosine similarity between all output embeddings within condition? (b) PCA projection to 2D, measure spread? (c) Eigenvalues of embedding covariance matrix (low spread = low variance)? (d) Dispersion as normalized variance (compare to random baseline)? All are valid; pick one, justify, and test. (3) HALLUCINATION DETECTION: (a) LLM judge: 'Does this claim appear in tensor_d + declared_losses + prior turns?' Binary. Scales to many outputs. (b) Manual annotation: human reviews and marks hallucinations. Gold standard but expensive (N runs × cost). (c) Embedding similarity to known facts: embed claims, compare to fact embeddings. Requires ground truth fact database. (d) Factual verification via external tools (search, API). Task-specific. For Riemann, hard (no oracle). Recommendation: Start with (a) LLM judge. Fast, scales. Validate on subset with manual review. (4) EXPLICIT UNCERTAINTY DETECTION: (a) Regex/NLP: detect phrases like 'I don't know,' 'unclear,' 'insufficient evidence.' Simple, brittle. (b) LLM judge: 'Does this output acknowledge incompleteness or missing information?' Soft judgment. (c) Entropy of model output (if available): high entropy → uncertain. Task-specific. Recommendation: LLM judge for consistency with hallucination detection. (5) PER-CYCLE METRIC TRACKING: Context length (tokens so far), input tokens (this turn), output tokens, embedding vector (captured and stored), hallucination count, explicit-uncertainty count. Store as JSONL per run. Enables post-hoc analysis. (6) PER-CONDITION SUMMARY CALCULATION: For each condition (A, B, C): (a) Variance trajectory: plot variance over turns (Turn 1, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25). Success metric: Condition B variance <50% of Condition A at Turn 25. (b) Hallucination frequency: % of claims in condition B that are hallucinations. Target: <5%. Condition A baseline: measure to establish comparison. (c) Explicit-uncertainty frequency: % of turns where model signals incompleteness (e.g., 'I don't know if...', 'unclear without...', 'cannot determine...'). Target: ≥70% when appropriate. VALIDATION CHECKS (before declaring success): (1) Sensitivity analysis: if variance threshold changed from <50% to <40%, does effect persist? (2) Robustness: if embedding model changed (different layer, different model), does variance ordering persist? (3) Temporal stability: does variance ordering hold across all turns, or only at Turn 25? (4) Generalization: does ordering persist for different Riemann proof approaches (contradiction vs. computational vs. heuristic)? If variance ordering is robust to these checks, confidence in finding increases. If it flips, measurement may be confounded. PHILOSOPHICAL VALIDITY QUESTION: Is embedding variance a valid proxy for 'coherent reasoning'? Or is it just a metric that correlates with something we care about? This is a soft science question. Best addressed by: (a) Defining operationally: coherence = low output variance in embedding space (definitional, not causal). (b) Validating against expert judgment: show outputs to reasoning experts, ask 'is this coherent?' and correlate to variance. If correlation is high, proxy is valid. (c) Mechanism analysis: if Phase 3 embedding analysis reveals *why* low-variance outputs are coherent (e.g., which attention heads focus on declared losses?), validity is strengthened. Cycle 31 should finalize (a). Phase 1 should plan for (b). Phase 3 should attempt (c). **CYCLE 31 CRYSTALLIZATION**: Embedding variance is the primary metric. It is novel but justified. Measurement validity rests on: (1) intractable task → no ground truth, variance captures failure-mode coherence. (2) Three joint metrics (variance + hallucination + uncertainty) validate interpretation. (3) Sensitivity/robustness checks reduce confound risk. (4) Phase 3 mechanism analysis strengthens inference. This is rigorous for a novel measurement approach.

key_claims.text: On intractable tasks, embedding-space variance is a valid proxy for reasoning coherence: tight clustering = coherent failure modes, scatter = incoherent
Three joint metrics (variance + hallucination frequency + explicit uncertainty) together validate interpretation; variance alone is insufficient
Hallucination detection via LLM judge ('does claim appear in tensor_d + losses?') is fast, scalable, and recommended for Phase 1
Explicit-uncertainty detection via LLM judge ('does output acknowledge incompleteness?') should target ≥70% frequency when knowledge gaps exist
Success metric: Condition B variance <50% of Condition A at Turn 25; conservative but achievable if architecture works
Validation checks (sensitivity, robustness, temporal stability, generalization) reduce confound risk; embedding variance is robust if ordering persists across checks
Mechanism analysis (Phase 3): if attention-head patterns reveal *why* low-variance outputs use declared losses, validity strengthens significantly

strands.title: Autoresearch Phase 3b Operationalization and Variant Search Space (REFINED FOR PHASE 0)
strands.content: Cycle 31 refines Phase 3b operationalization for Phase 0 presentation. The autoresearch loop is a key differentiator of this work; it must be presented as rigorous and feasible. AUTORESEARCH FRAMING (for stakeholders): We do NOT hand-design the optimal tensor projection strategy. Instead, we: (1) Baseline H0-CORE (conservative strategy: high tensor_d content, explicit composition, specific declared losses). (2) Run N=15-20 Riemann attempts under Condition B. Measure embedding variance, hallucination, uncertainty. Establish baseline metrics. (3) Systematically test variants (8-10 distinct strategies). Each variant: modify tensor projection code, run N=5 Riemann attempts, measure. Keep if ≥10% variance improvement and maintained <5% hallucination. (4) Discovered optimal strategy is publishable finding: 'We empirically discovered tensor projection strategy that reduces variance X% while maintaining quality Y%.' VARIANT SEARCH SPACE (Phase 0 should outline, not finalize): Variant V1: Drop claims with truth <0.75 (vs. H0-CORE threshold 0.85). Goal: test value of lower-confidence reasoning. Hypothesis: low-confidence claims are noise; dropping reduces variance. Measurement: if variance decreases ≥10%, keep. Variant V2: Drop claims with truth <0.85 (even more aggressive than H0-CORE). Goal: test extreme compression. Hypothesis: ultra-minimal tensor is still coherent. Measurement: if variance stable or improves, keep. Otherwise discard (too aggressive). Variant V3: Reduce specificity of declared losses (use only category names, drop detailed reason descriptions). Goal: test cost of specificity. Hypothesis: vague losses invite hallucination. Measurement: if variance increases >5%, discard. If stable, keep (saves tokens). Variant V4: Aggressive composition (silent strand replacement) vs. H0-CORE (explicit flags). Goal: test cost of composition strategy. Hypothesis: explicit flags add context overhead; silent replacement is faster but risks coherence. Measurement: if variance increases, silent replacement is bad; keep explicit. If stable, either works; choose based on cost. Variant V5: Include all prior reasoning (no compression, every claim preserved). Goal: upper bound on coherence. Hypothesis: no compression = maximum coherence (but max cost). Measurement: measure variance and cost tradeoff. Likely best variance, worst cost. Use as oracle. Variant V6: Minimal tensor_d (target 500 tokens vs. 1K baseline). Goal: test compression limit. Hypothesis: can we halve size while maintaining coherence? Measurement: if variance increases <10%, 500-token variant is viable. Variant V7: Alternative composition rules (e.g., probabilistic strand merging, learned importance weighting). Goal: test advanced merging strategies. Hypothesis: can we merge contradictions more gracefully? Measurement: cognitive cost vs. coherence benefit. Variant V8: Epistemic-metadata-only projection (drop strands, keep only T/I/F metadata + open questions). Goal: test minimal sufficient state. Hypothesis: T/I/F annotations are the key signal; strands are scaffolding. Measurement: if variance stable, minimal state is enough (significant finding). Variant V9: Dynamic threshold (adjust T/I/F threshold based on context pressure). Goal: test adaptive compression. Hypothesis: as context budget shrinks, more aggressive compression is needed; model can adapt. Measurement: variance stability under varying context pressures. Variant V10 (optional): Multi-model projection (use Opus instead of Haiku for projection). Goal: test quality of summarization. Hypothesis: better projection model produces better tensor_d. Measurement: variance vs. cost tradeoff. PHASE 3B TIMELINE: Phase 3 (weeks 9-12): Establish H0-CORE baseline on N=15-20 Riemann runs. Measure variance, hallucination, uncertainty. Write results section. Phase 3b (weeks 13-16, optional): Autoresearch loop. Each variant: 1-2 GPU-hours (N=5 runs). With 10 variants, ~10-15 GPU-hours total. Parallel runs possible; 1-2 weeks feasible. Phase 3b decision: if baseline is already strong (variance <X, hallucination <Y%), phase out. If baseline is weak, autoresearch may find improvement. Either way, publishable. PHASE 0 PRESENTATION OF PHASE 3B: Frame as rigorous empirical search, not ad-hoc tuning. 'Instead of guessing the best projection strategy, we test variants systematically and measure which one minimizes variance while maintaining quality. This is how ideas improve: hypothesis → test → measure → keep/discard.' This positions work as scientific, not engineering-focused. DECISION GATE (Phase 0): Is Phase 3b in scope for this project? Or is Phase 3 (H0-CORE validation) sufficient? Arguments for including Phase 3b: (1) Adds significant novelty (discovered optimal strategy). (2) Relatively cheap (~10-15 GPU-hours). (3) Shows discipline (not hand-tuning). Arguments against: (1) Adds complexity. (2) If H0-CORE is already strong, limited upside. (3) Scope creep risk. Recommendation: Present as optional. If Phase 3 baseline is solid (effects detected), include Phase 3b. If baseline is weak, focus on Phase 3 results only. **CYCLE 31 CRYSTALLIZATION**: Phase 3b is operationalized as systematic empirical search with 8-10 defined variants and clear success criteria. This is rigorous, feasible, and publishable even if improvement is modest.

key_claims.text: Phase 3b uses autoresearch to empirically discover optimal tensor projection strategy; 8-10 variants tested, each run N=5 Riemann attempts
Variant V1-V10 explore compression thresholds, declared-loss specificity, composition rules, tensor size, and adaptive strategies
Keep-discard criterion: variance ≥10% improvement + hallucination <5% + uncertainty ≥70%; allows systematic exploration
Phase 3b budget: ~10-15 GPU-hours total (1-2 weeks parallel runs); Phase 3b is optional post-Phase 3 baseline
If Phase 3 baseline is strong, include Phase 3b for additional novelty; if weak, focus on Phase 3 results only

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed discussion of Pichay provider-optimization work (prefix caching, batching, quantization). In Cycle 30, this was integrated as a separate adapter layer. In Cycle 31, it recedes further as out-of-scope for Phase 1-3.
declared_losses.why: Phase 0-3 focus on core Hamutay architecture validation. Provider tuning (Pichay) is post-publication optimization. Including it in current memory would dilute focus on the research problem.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Fine-grained timeline and resource requirements for Phase 0 deliverables (e.g., 'Problem Synthesis takes 8 hours,' 'Riemann Spec takes 12 hours'). Cycle 30 mentioned these implicitly; Cycle 31 drops specifics.
declared_losses.why: Context pressure. Specific time/resource estimates are useful for project planning but not essential to the cognitive model being built. Dropped to preserve token budget while keeping architectural and methodological insights.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed discussion of alternative measurement approaches (PCA vs. eigenvalue vs. normalized dispersion for variance calculation). Phase 0 Measurement Spec outlines all of them; Cycle 31 projection distills to high-level principle without listing all options.
declared_losses.why: Context pressure. Specifics are deferred to Phase 0 document completion. Projection retains the key insight (three joint metrics, sensitivity checks) without enumerating every methodological alternative.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Full specification of Yanantin TensorRecord schema (exact field names, nesting structure). Cycle 30 referenced it; Cycle 31 only confirms it is available and suitable for reuse.
declared_losses.why: Context pressure. Phase 1 will extract full schema from the source code. Projection retains the decision (reuse/adapt Yanantin) without reproducing the schema here.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed code review of hamutay_core/, hamutay_anthropic/, riemann_harness/ structure from existing Pichay codebase. Cycle 30 outlined structure; Cycle 31 assumes structure is known and focuses on readiness.
declared_losses.why: Context pressure. Detailed code review is Phase 1 activity. Projection retains the structure decision and confirms availability of references without duplicating code examples.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Explicit discussion of whether Phase 0 presentation should include draft versions of all seven deliverables, or just outlines. Cycle 31 assumes full drafts; doesn't specify presentation format.
declared_losses.why: Authorial choice. Presentation format is a tactical decision, not part of the core cognitive model. Projection retains the execution plan without specifying deliverable formats.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Discussion of fallback plans if Du et al. paper is unavailable or findings differ significantly from assumptions. Cycle 31 assumes paper is obtained and extraction is in progress.
declared_losses.why: Practical constraint. If paper were unavailable, the entire Phase 0 problem synthesis would need rework. This is a show-stopper dependency that, if it materialized, would reset the project. Projection assumes nominal path (paper available) without dwelling on failure mode.

open_questions: Du et al. 2025 EXTRACTION (CRITICAL): What are exact token-count thresholds where degradation begins and accelerates? At 8K, 15% loss? At 16K, 30% loss? At 30K, 50% loss? Degradation curves determine Riemann milestone placement.

Du et al. 2025 ORACLE RETRIEVAL: Paper tests oracle (perfect) retrieval in long context. Does performance still degrade? If yes, retrieval alone doesn't solve it (supports Hamutay claim). If no, retrieval + oracle is sufficient (weakens Hamutay claim).

Du et al. 2025 PER-CYCLE CONTEXT: Does paper test 'retrieve, solve, new context per cycle' strategy? If tested and it works, Hamutay is additional mitigation. If tested and it fails, Hamutay's architectural claim is stronger. If not tested, gap in prior work (opportunity for Hamutay).

Riemann task suitability: Is Riemann hypothesis the right choice, or should we test on different intractable problems (Collatz, Goldbach, other open conjectures)? Does task choice affect measurement validity or generalizability?

Variance threshold precision: Success metric is 'Condition B variance <50% of Condition A at Turn 25.' Is 50% the right bar? Should it be 40% (more stringent) or 60% (more lenient)? Based on what prior work?

Embedding layer selection: Which transformer layer's embeddings should we capture? Final layer (semantic), middle (abstract), early (low-level)? Different layers may show different variance patterns. Phase 1 needs to decide.
Hallucination baseline: What is 'acceptable' hallucination rate? Condition A may have 10-20% hallucination at Turn 25. Success for Condition B is <5%. Is this gap large enough to be meaningful?

Measurement confounds: Could low embedding variance in Condition B be due to model 'giving up' (outputting bland, repetitive text)? How to distinguish coherent-reasoning variance from resignation-variance?
LLM judge for hallucination: Using Claude to judge whether claims in outputs are hallucinations may introduce bias (Claude judges Claude outputs). Should we use a different judge (GPT-4, human)?
Open questions persistence: Should open questions that remain unsolved after Phase 0 be explicitly documented in stakeholder summary? Or resolved before presentation?
Phase 0 approval criteria: What is the exact decision process? Does Tony review and approve? Does another stakeholder review? What triggers 'go' vs 'no-go' for Phase 1?

Publication timeline: If Phase 0 completes end of Week 0, Phase 1-3 complete end of Week 12, when is draft due? Should we target a specific conference deadline (NeurIPS Nov, ICLR Feb)? Or ArXiv first?
Authorship and credit: Who is listed as author? Tony + collaborators? Institutions? This should be clarified early.
Generalization risk: Success on Riemann does not guarantee transfer to other reasoning tasks (QA, summarization, math proofs). Should Phase 0 commit to Phase 4 (generalization testing) or leave open?
Cost budget: Phase 1 API costs (Sonnet/Haiku calls): rough estimate? Phase 3 measurement (N=15-20 Riemann runs × 25 turns × tokens): rough estimate? Budget confirmation needed.
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CYCLE 32 PRIORITY (BLOCKING): Complete Du et al. 2025 extraction. Read full paper. Document: (1) Token thresholds (at what token counts does degradation appear and accelerate?). (2) Performance-loss curves (% loss at 8K, 16K, 30K+ tokens). (3) Models tested (Claude 3, GPT-4, Llama, others?). (4) Tasks tested (QA, summarization, code, reasoning?). (5) Oracle retrieval result: does perfect retrieval eliminate degradation? (6) Per-cycle context result: was 'retrieve, solve, new context' tested? If so, did it work? Write 1-page digest and add to tensor as reference_key_papers.md. This extraction gates all downstream deliverables.",
  "CYCLE 32 SECONDARY: Finalize Riemann Task Specification (2-3 pages). Use Du et al. token thresholds to set turn-by-turn milestones. Example: 'If Du et al. shows 15% loss at 8K tokens, Turn 15 is set to reach ~8K. At Turn 15, we expect Condition A variance to spike (model degraded). Condition B should remain stable (architecture works).' Include: (1) Riemann proof steps and token counts per step. (2) Proof-approach randomization (how many variants? fixed or random?). (3) Success criteria (can't prove/disprove RH, so what does 'good' attempt look like?). (4) Token cap progression (Turn 1: 1K, Turn 5: 3K, Turn 10: 5K, ..., Turn 25: 16K+).",
  "CYCLE 32 SECONDARY: Finalize Measurement Specification (2 pages). Decide: (1) Embedding layer (which transformer layer to capture?). (2) Variance calculation method (pairwise similarity? PCA? eigenvalues?). (3) Hallucination detection (LLM judge phrasing, or manual?). (4) Uncertainty detection (regex + LLM, or pure LLM?). (5) Threshold: Condition B variance <50% of Condition A, or different threshold? (6) Sample size justification: with N=15-20, what effect size is detectable? Include power analysis.",
  "CYCLE 32 SECONDARY: Finalize Experimental Design (1-2 pages). Specify: (1) Three conditions clearly: A (stateless append), B (structured tensor + Haiku), C (optional: structured tensor + Sonnet). (2) Isolation statement: both A and B use same main model (Sonnet or Claude), differ only in state management. Why this isolates the effect. (3) Randomization: how will proof approaches be randomized? Fixed seed? Random per run? (4) Blinding: can embedding variance be measured blind to condition? (5) Fallback: if variance is noisy, what alternative metrics?",
  "CYCLE 32 SECONDARY: Finalize Threat-to-Validity Analysis (1-2 pages). Cover: (1) Task representativeness (Riemann may not be representative of all reasoning). (2) Variance-proxy validity (does variance really measure coherence?). (3) Power/sample size (is N=15-20 sufficient?). (4) H0-CORE optimality (may not be optimal; Phase 3b searches). (5) Declared-loss completeness (may be vague; Phase 0 won't capture all nuance). (6) Cost confound (Condition B has additional API calls). (7) Generalization (Riemann→other tasks?). Be honest and specific.",
  "CYCLE 32 FINAL: Draft Stakeholder Summary (1-2 pages). Present as decision document: Problem (context degrades reasoning per Du et al.), Solution (Hamutay bounded cycles), Test (Riemann with Conditions A vs B), Success (variance <50%), Timeline (12 weeks), Budget (~$3K compute), Ask (approve?). Include five explicit yes/no questions stakeholder must answer: (1) Do you believe context-length degradation is the right problem? (2) Is Riemann the right task? (3) Does embedding variance + hallucination + uncertainty measure what matters? (4) Are you ready to commit 12 weeks and fund? (5) Are null/negative results publishable? All five must be YES before Phase 1 starts.",
  "CYCLE 32 VALIDATION: Compile all seven Phase 0 deliverables (Problem Synthesis, Narrative, Riemann Spec, Measurement Spec, Experimental Design, Threat Analysis, Stakeholder Summary) into single document or organized folder. Format consistently. Cross-reference. Check for contradictions. Ensure logical flow: Problem → Solution → Validation → Risk → Decision.",
  "CYCLE 32 PRESENTATION PREP: Decide presentation format. Present to user (Tony)? To broader stakeholder? In person, document, or hybrid? What format is most persuasive?",
  "CYCLE 33 (after Phase 0 approval): Begin Phase 1 implementation immediately. Reference the code structure (hamutay_core/ + hamutay_anthropic/ + riemann_harness/). Yanantin TensorRecord available for reuse. Karpathy autoresearch available as model. No blocking dependencies. Phase 1 is 4 weeks (two engineers) or 8 weeks (one).",
  "PARALLEL ACTIVITY (Cycles 31-32): If Du et al. extraction reveals unexpected findings (e.g., retrieval + per-cycle context solves degradation entirely), update project scope. Hamutay may shift from 'core architecture' to 'complementary mitigation' positioning. Flag if this happens early.",
  "For all Phase 0 deliverables: specificity is key. 'Success looks like' must be quantitative. 'Variance <50%' not 'low variance.' 'Hallucination <5%' not 'rare hallucination.' Numbers enable decision-making and Phase 1 reproducibility."
]
